$meta.name: Michael J. Gauthier Design System
$meta.mission: Encouraging others to use their God-given resources for God-given purposes
$meta.description: Machine-readable design tokens for Hermes, Claude, and ChatGPT agents producing landing pages, email, social, blog, print, and branding for MJG.
$meta.usage: Apply data-theme="light" or "dark" on the root. Reference CSS vars (mjg-tokens.css) or these raw values.

designLanguage.summary: Warm editorial. Cream paper + near-black ink in light; true near-black in dark. GOLD is an accent — used for italic display words, eyebrows, and a single gold CTA — never the default button color.
designLanguage.primaryAction: Solid near-black button (light) / cream button (dark). Gold is the secondary 'movement' CTA.
designLanguage.headings: High-contrast serif (Playfair Display), often with a gold italic emphasis word.
designLanguage.backgrounds: Light = warm paper #F4F1EA with faint blueprint grid. Dark = near-black #0C0C0C.

usage.display: Headlines, titles, dollar figures. High contrast. Use italic + gold for the emphasis word (The Stewardship *Blueprint*, $1 *Billion*).
usage.sans: Body copy, UI, labels, buttons, eyebrows (uppercase + letter-spaced).

buttons.primary: background primary (#161616 light / #F4F1EA dark), foreground primary-foreground. Radius full or md. The main CTA.
buttons.gold: background gold #C5A468, white text. The 'Join the Movement' / 'Contact Us' CTA. Use sparingly, one per view.

components.audioPlayer: Eyebrow label, thin scrubber with gold dot handle + times, a dark rounded pill bar holding bookmark / rewind-10 / large gold play-pause circle / forward-10 / restart icons, and a gold volume slider below-right. Credit line beneath: 'By Michael J. Gauthier · Life Design · Stewardship · Faith'.

logos.lockup: Monogram + 'Michael J. Gauthier' set in italic Playfair, with a gold 'J.'
rules: Light backgrounds: black or teal monogram. Dark backgrounds: white or gold monogram.
Maintain clear space equal to the height of the 'M'.
Never recolor, stretch, add shadows, or place the mark on low-contrast fields.

principles: Speak to design, not accident — 'living by design, not by default'.
Integrate faith, family, finances, and legacy; never preachy.
Clarity over cleverness. Short, declarative sentences.
Invite, don't push — 'Join the journey', not 'Buy now'.
Stewardship framing: resources are entrusted, to be used on purpose.
do: Use building/blueprint metaphors (bedrock, foundation, pillars, guardrails, legacy)
Write in first person for Michael's voice
dont: No hype, urgency-bait, or prosperity-gospel language
Avoid exclamation overload and emoji in formal contexts
